content.subtitle: Structure
items: if/when/whenever + present tense, present tense

content.subtitle: Uses
items: General or scientific facts and definitions

content.subtitle: Example
items: If you burn fossil fuels, carbon dioxide is produced.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + present tense, will + bare infinitive / imperative

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Real conditions in the present or future and their results in the present or future
To give conditional instructions

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If we continue to pollute our planet, future generations will suffer.
If the people from Greenpeace call, tell them I'll call them back later.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: Other modals and semi-modals can be used instead of will in the result clause in first conditionals. These include be going to, can, could, may, might, shall, should, have to and ought to.
examples: We might prevent disaster if we change the way we live now.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + past simple / past continuous, would + bare infinitive

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Hypothetical conditions in the present or future and their hypothetical results in the present or future

content.subtitle: Example
items: What would the local government do if there was an earthquake in the area?

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: Other modals can be used instead of would in the result clause in second conditionals. These include might and could.
examples: If the weather was better, we could have a picnic.

content.subtitle: Grammar Note
content.description: In British English, we can use both was and were after if with first and third person singular. Were is more common in a formal style. In American English, it is usual to use were. In both British and American English, were is usually used in the phrase 'if I were you, ...'
examples: UK: If I was/were a gambler, I'd put money on Jim being late.
US: If I were a gambler, I'd put money on Jim being late.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + past perfect, would have + past participle

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Hypothetical conditions in the past and their results in the past

content.subtitle: Example
items: A lot more people would have been trapped by the flood if there hadn't been a warning.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: Other modals can be used instead of would in the result clause in third conditionals. These include might, could and should.
examples: If you hadn't had a lot of luck, you could have lost all your money.

content.subtitle: Structure and Uses
items: Hypothetical conditions in the past and their results in the present
Hypothetical conditions in the present and their results in the past

content.subtitle: Examples
items: You might not be in so much trouble if you hadn't started gambling.
If I were you, I would have made Paula buy her own lottery ticket.

content.subtitle: First Conditional (formal)
examples: Should the drought continue, many people will be forced to leave their villages. (= if the drought should continue … / If the drought continues …)

content.subtitle: Second Conditional (formal)
examples: Were we to stop using fossil fuels tomorrow, it would still take decades for the planet to recover. (= If we were to stop … / If we stopped …)

content.subtitle: Third Conditional (formal)
examples: Had Charles Darwin not visited the Galapagos Islands, he might never have developed his theory of evolution. (= If Charles Darwin hadn't visited …)

content.subtitle: Negative Inversion
content.description: When the verb to be inverted is negative, we put not after the subject.
examples: Had we not attended the meeting, we would have had no idea of the council's plans.

content.subtitle: Comma Usage
content.description: When the if clause comes before the result clause, we usually separate the two clauses with a comma. When the result clause comes first, we do not use a comma.
examples: If you share a car to work, you can save on energy.
You can save on energy if you share a car to work.

content.subtitle: Alternative Condition Words
items: as/so long as
provided/providing (that)
on condition (that)
suppose/supposing
if it wasn't/weren't for + noun
were it not for + noun
but for + noun
if it hadn't been for + noun
had it not been for + noun
if … (should) happen to
if … should
if … happened to
if … were to
if so/not
otherwise
unless
in case of + noun
in case

content.subtitle: Examples
items: We'll go up to the mountains this weekend as long as the weather's okay.
You can have a pet provided that you promise to look after it properly.
Applications for membership are accepted on condition that applicants are over 18.
Supposing the price of oil tripled tomorrow. What do you think would happen?
I think I'd be quite lonely if it wasn't/weren't for my dog, Buster.
Were it not for my dog, Buster, I think I'd be quite lonely.
But for your help, I wouldn't have been able to quit gambling.
If it hadn't been for your help, I wouldn't have been able to quit gambling.
Had it not been for your help, I wouldn't have been able to quit gambling.
If you (should) happen to see Davina, ask her whether she would look after the cats this weekend.
If you should see Davina, ask her whether she would look after the cats this weekend.
If you happened to see someone drop litter in the street, what would you do?
Do you think it would reduce pollution if the government were to introduce a new tax on petrol?
Are you concerned about the environment? If so, you might be interested in joining Greenpeace.
You should have your air conditioner serviced, otherwise you'll waste a lot of energy.
Unless governments act now, the environment is really going to suffer.
In case of fire, leave the building by the nearest emergency exit.
Take a coat with you in case the weather gets worse.

content.subtitle: Meanings
items: To mean 'if it is true that'
To mean 'if you are willing to'
To mean 'I'm saying this in case'

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If you were at the meeting, why didn't you raise the issue of recycling?
If you'll follow me, I'll show you into the park manager's office.
If you like zoos, the one in Singapore is fantastic.

sections.title: A: Match to Make Sentences
sections.instructions: Match the beginnings (1-10) with the endings (A-J) to make complete conditional sentences.

items.sentence: If you stay with us for three days,
If you decide not to vote in the election,
If you didn't eat your lunch so fast,
If there were a few more people working,
If you forget to take your passport,
If the hire car isn't there when you arrive,
If the city continues to expand,
If the police didn't fine people,
If you did decide to leave the company,
If MPs wish to resign,

sections.title: B: Rewrite Using Conditionals
sections.instructions: Rewrite the sentences using an appropriate conditional form, starting with the words given.

items.sentence: Sue didn't get me a birthday present, which is why I didn't get her one.
items.answer: If Sue had got me a birthday present, I would have got her one.

items.sentence: We didn't pay them on time because they made a mistake with our order.
items.answer: If they hadn't made a mistake with our order, we would have paid them on time.

items.sentence: The climate is undergoing such radical changes that scientists are worried.
items.answer: If the climate weren't undergoing such radical changes, scientists wouldn't be worried.

items.sentence: Did California become an important area because they found gold there?
items.answer: Would California have become an important area if they hadn't found gold there?

items.sentence: It's the sunset that attracts tourists to that place.
items.answer: If it weren't for the sunset, tourists wouldn't be attracted to that place.

items.sentence: There was a traffic jam on the motorway so I was late for my interview.
items.answer: If there hadn't been a traffic jam on the motorway, I wouldn't have been late for my interview.

items.sentence: I suggest you use a sunblock, and then you won't go red.
items.answer: If I were you, I would use a sunblock so that you don't go red.

items.sentence: The accident happened as a result of the driver's not paying attention to the road.
items.answer: If the driver had paid attention to the road, the accident wouldn't have happened.

sections.title: C: Complete the Text
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in bold. Add any other words you need.

items.sentence: If I (1) ...... (do) that then,
I never (2) ...... (have) so many fantastic experiences.
If you (3) ...... (ask) me now,
if any (4) ...... (book).
I (5) ...... (have) time to check
especially if visitors to the park (6) ...... (get) lost
which wouldn't (7) ...... (happen)
if the woman involved (8) ...... (be) a bit more careful.
if you (9) ...... (wander) around
This particular case might (10) ...... (be) so bad
if it (11) ...... (start) raining.
I dread to think what might (12) ...... (happen) if we hadn't!

sections.title: D: Circle the Correct Word or Phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: If we hadn't taken a wrong turning back there, we wouldn't be / have been in this mess now.
Should / Would you need any further assistance, please don't hesitate to ask.
Had they not / Hadn't they warned people in time, a lot more lives could have been lost.
If you worked / had worked last weekend when the boss asked you, then you wouldn't have to work this weekend.
Were all the members of staff to attend the conference, would the hotel have / have had enough rooms for everyone?
Had the government acted sooner, the general public wouldn't be worrying / have worried so much about the current situation.
Tom Cruise wouldn't be the star he is today had he not / should he not have made a good impression in his early films.
Weren't we / Were we not to build more roads, the traffic system would collapse by 2020.
It would be hard enough to pass the exam tomorrow even if you went / had gone to all the lectures this year.
You can contact the hotel manager on extension 142 should / could you need to.
Shouldn't you / Should you not receive confirmation of your flight by e-mail, please click the link below.
I would have asked the person for ID before I let him in, if I were / have been you.

sections.title: E: Complete with the Words in the Box
sections.instructions: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: Do you want a highly-paid career, and, if ...... , are you prepared to retrain?
I'm quite happy to lend you money, as ...... as you make sure I get it back.
The village fair should be a great success, ...... we don't have the same problems with the weather as last year.
It'd be great to see you on Saturday evening, ...... you've already got other plans.
Could you go to the shop for me? I have to stay in in ...... the courier comes.
Your idea sounds fine in theory, but just ...... something goes wrong. What then?
Write your name at the top of your essay, ...... I won't know which one's yours.
The judge allowed the accused to stay at home on ...... she reported to the police every morning.

sections.title: F: Write One Word in Each Gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I would never be able to afford a new house if ...... wasn't for all the overtime I've been doing.
If it hadn't ...... for the fact that his uncle was a partner in the business, Angus would never have got the job.
How do you think you'd feel if someone happened ...... damage your car like that?
...... for the kindness of a complete stranger, I would never have found the hotel.
I don't think I'd be surprised in the slightest ...... Paul were to suddenly announce that he was moving abroad.
Don't forget to mention the new schedule to Veronica if you ...... happen to see her this evening.
Football matches would be ideal family outings ...... it not for the danger of violence.
If it hadn't been ...... the children, I'm sure Trisha would have left Tom a long time ago.
I would have lost the game ...... it not been for some quick thinking.
If you ...... find the book I was asking you about, give me a call.

sections.title: G: Rewrite the Sentences
sections.instructions: Rewrite the sentences, starting with the words given.

items.sentence: The only reason we won the contract was Debbie's fantastic presentation.
items.answer: We wouldn't have won the contract if it hadn't been for Debbie's fantastic presentation.

items.sentence: The eventual arrival of the ferry prevented a fight from breaking out amongst the passengers.
items.answer: If it hadn't been for the eventual arrival of the ferry, a fight would have broken out amongst the passengers.

items.sentence: None of the passengers was injured because of the driver's quick reaction.
items.answer: But for the driver's quick reaction, some of the passengers would have been injured.

items.sentence: We'll have to cancel the meeting if Wendy doesn't turn up soon.
items.answer: Unless Wendy turns up soon, we'll have to cancel the meeting.

items.sentence: Mr Jones would be quite lonely if he didn't have his daughter's visits.
items.answer: Were it not for his daughter's visits, Mr Jones would be quite lonely.

items.sentence: Here's my phone number, because you might need it.
items.answer: Here's my phone number in case you need it.

items.sentence: Agree to be back by midnight and you can go.
items.answer: Providing you agree to be back by midnight, you can go.

items.sentence: Officer Hughes prevented the robbers from escaping.
items.answer: Had it not been for Officer Hughes, the robbers would have escaped.

sections.title: H: Choose the Correct Answer
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer A or B.

items.sentence: If you saw another student cheating in an exam, ...... somebody?
Make sure you mix the ingredients well, ...... you might get lumps in your cake.
Were ...... my dad, I would never have started playing tennis in the first place.
If I ...... hear from Nigel, I'll tell him you were asking after him.
The book would have been perfect ...... the ending.
If you hadn't taken the money, you ...... in prison now.
Do you think that if we ...... earlier we might not have missed the coach?
Unless Ray ...... here soon, I'm going to leave because I'm getting bored.
What if I ...... you that there's a good chance I can get tickets for the concert?
If there ...... happen to be any reason for late delivery, please inform our Accounts Department.

sections.title: I: CFCs Cloze Text
sections.instructions: Complete the text with the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Had (1) ...... not been for the invention of CFCs
In fact, (2) ...... they to be invented today,
they (3) ...... no doubt be immediately banned.
there (4) ...... a good chance that it contained CFCs
If you (5) ...... able to travel into the upper layers
you (6) ...... see the chlorine and fluorine
Ozone could damage your lungs (7) ...... you were to breathe it,
if you (8) ...... exposed to them.
We might (9) ...... have known about the process
(10) ...... it not been for the work of two American scientists
and might have won (11) ...... the evidence not been so clear.
but (12) ...... which we might today be facing an even greater problem.

sections.title: J: Keyword Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence: Without this new diet, Henry would never have lost so much weight.
items.answer: Had it not been for this new diet, Henry would never have lost so much weight.

items.sentence: Unless the plans change, we'll see you on the 19th.
items.answer: Provided there is no change to the plans, we'll see you on the 19th.

items.sentence: If you applied now, there's a good chance you would get the job.
items.answer: Were you to apply now, there's a good chance you would get the job.

items.sentence: I didn't know about your problem so I didn't offer to help.
items.answer: If I'd known about your problem, I would have offered to help.

items.sentence: It could get cold at night, so take some warm clothes.
items.answer: Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold at night.

items.sentence: I'd love to travel the world if I didn't have to pay off my mortgage.
items.answer: Were it not for having to pay off my mortgage, I'd love to travel the world.

items.sentence: Ten years ago, people would have complained at seeing this film.
items.answer: Had people seen this film ten years ago, they would have complained.

items.sentence: Drivers are allowed to park here if they display a local resident permit.
items.answer: Drivers are allowed to park here on condition that they display a local resident permit.
